elements.name: Support chat widget
elements.notes: Live chat embedded in the product. Serves tier-1 queries and escalates to human agents.

elements.name: Customer reports an issue
elements.notes: Customer describes the problem via chat or email. Quality of description affects triage speed.

elements.name: Ticket routing system
elements.notes: Tags incoming tickets by topic and urgency, then assigns to the right agent queue.

elements.name: Support agent reviews and triages ticket
elements.notes: Agent reads customer message, tags priority, and either resolves immediately or escalates.

elements.name: Incorrect triage causing delay
elements.notes: Mis-tagged tickets end up in the wrong queue, adding 4–8 hours before reassignment.

elements.name: Internal knowledge base
elements.notes: Searchable runbooks and known-issue articles used by agents to resolve tier-2 queries.

elements.name: Agent communicates resolution
elements.notes: Agent explains fix to customer via chat or email and confirms the issue is resolved.

elements.name: Resolution confirmation email
elements.notes: Sent to the customer when their ticket is marked resolved. Includes summary and re-open link.

elements.name: Customer evaluates whether issue is truly resolved
elements.notes: The customer's verdict — whether to re-open or accept resolution — determines satisfaction and NPS.

meta.reason: directly drives CSAT and re-contact rate

elements.name: CSAT survey
elements.notes: One-question satisfaction rating sent 24 hours after resolution. Target: >85% positive.

elements.name: NPS and resolution-time dashboard
elements.notes: Weekly report of NPS score, average time-to-resolution, and repeat-contact rate by category.
